gaps: No test covers the scenario where a newly paired headset (via phone Bluetooth settings, not via app) fails to appear in the Poly Studio Mobile app device list without an app restart.

No test covers the scenario where a device that is displayed in the device list but powered off fails to update its status to 'Connected' when the device is powered on, without manual refresh or app restart.
No test covers the Bluetooth state transition of 'device powered off -> powered on' and whether the app's device status updates reactively.
No test covers the app's failure to detect a newly paired Bluetooth device via the system Bluetooth pairing flow (external to the app) and reflect it in the device list without app lifecycle restart.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BT-DISCOVERY-001: Newly Paired Headset via Phone Bluetooth Settings Does Not Appear in App Device List Without App Restart
steps: Precondition: Headset is fully charged, powered off, and not currently paired to the phone.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
Navigate to the main Device List screen. Verify no headset is listed (or only previously paired devices).
Minimize the app (press Home button) but do not close it.
Open phone Settings → Bluetooth.
Put the headset into pairing mode (e.g., slide and hold Power switch away from off position until LCDs flash red and blue).
From the phone's Bluetooth settings, select the headset from 'Available devices' to pair. Confirm pairing is successful (status shows 'Connected' or 'Paired').
Return to Poly Studio Mobile app by tapping the app icon or switching to it from the recent apps list.
Observe the Device List screen in the app.
Do NOT restart the app. Do NOT pull-to-refresh or tap any refresh button.
Verify whether the newly paired headset appears in the app's device list.
generated_testcases.expected: The newly paired headset SHOULD appear in the Poly Studio Mobile app device list automatically without requiring an app restart. If the headset does not appear, this is a bug indicating the app fails to detect system Bluetooth pairing events.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BT-DISCOVERY-002: Newly Paired Headset Persists in App Device List After App Force-Kill and Restart
steps: Precondition: Headset is paired to the phone via phone Bluetooth settings (not via app). Headset is powered ON and connected.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Verify the headset is NOT visible in the device list (baseline for bug reproduction).
Force-kill the app completely (swipe from recent apps).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to the main Device List screen.
Observe whether the headset now appears in the device list.
generated_testcases.expected: After app force-kill and restart, the newly paired headset should appear in the device list. If it appears only after restart, this confirms the bug that the app does not dynamically detect new Bluetooth pairings.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BT-DISCOVERY-003: Device Status Fails to Update from 'Disconnected' to 'Connected' When Powered On Without App Restart
steps: Precondition: Headset is already paired and visible in the Poly Studio Mobile app device list.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate to the main Device List screen.
Verify the headset is listed and currently shows status as 'Connected' (power it on and let it connect first).
Power OFF the headset (slide Power switch to OFF position).
Wait 10 seconds. Observe the device status in the app updates to 'Disconnected' or the device shows as unavailable.
Do NOT restart the app. Do NOT navigate away or pull-to-refresh.
Power ON the headset (slide Power switch to ON position).
Wait up to 30 seconds for Bluetooth reconnection.
Observe the device status in the app's Device List screen.
Check if the status automatically updates to 'Connected' without any manual intervention.
generated_testcases.expected: The app should reactively update the device status from 'Disconnected' to 'Connected' when the headset is powered on and reconnects via Bluetooth, without requiring an app restart or manual refresh.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BT-DISCOVERY-004: Device Status Update After Power Cycle with App Backgrounding
steps: Precondition: Headset is paired and visible in the Poly Studio Mobile app device list. Headset is powered ON and connected.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate to the main Device List screen.
Verify the headset shows status 'Connected'.
Power OFF the headset.
Immediately press the Home button to background the app.
Wait 15 seconds.
Power ON the headset.
Wait 10 seconds for reconnection.
Bring the Poly Studio Mobile app back to the foreground (tap app icon or switch from recent apps).
Observe the device status on the Device List screen.
generated_testcases.expected: The device status should update to 'Connected' after the headset is powered on and reconnects, even when the app was backgrounded during the power cycle. The app should detect the state change upon returning to the foreground.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BT-DISCOVERY-005: Device Status Update After Power Cycle with App in Foreground - No Manual Refresh
steps: Precondition: Headset is paired and visible in the Poly Studio Mobile app device list. Headset is powered ON and connected.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate to the main Device List screen.
Verify the headset shows status 'Connected'.
Keep the app in the foreground. Do NOT navigate away.
Power OFF the headset.
Observe the device status on screen. Note whether it changes to 'Disconnected' within 15 seconds.
Power ON the headset.
Observe the device status on screen continuously for up to 30 seconds.
Do NOT tap any refresh button, do NOT navigate away, do NOT restart the app.
Record whether the status automatically transitions from 'Disconnected' to 'Connected'.
generated_testcases.expected: The app should reactively update the device status from 'Connected' → 'Disconnected' (when powered off) and then from 'Disconnected' → 'Connected' (when powered on) in real-time without any manual refresh or app restart.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BT-DISCOVERY-006: Rapid Power Cycle - Device Status Consistency After Multiple Off/On Transitions
steps: Precondition: Headset is paired and visible in the Poly Studio Mobile app device list. Headset is powered ON and connected.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate to the main Device List screen.
Verify the headset shows status 'Connected'.
Power OFF the headset. Wait for status to show 'Disconnected'.
Within 5 seconds of seeing 'Disconnected', power ON the headset.
Wait 15 seconds. Observe the status.
Immediately power OFF the headset again.
Wait 5 seconds, then power ON the headset.
Wait 20 seconds.
Record the final device status displayed in the app.
generated_testcases.expected: After rapid power cycles, the app should correctly reflect the final state of the headset. If the headset is ON and connected, status should show 'Connected'. The app should not get stuck in a 'Disconnected' state or show stale status after multiple rapid transitions.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BT-DISCOVERY-007: Device Status Update After Power On When App Was Force-Killed During Power-Off State
steps: Precondition: Headset is paired and visible in the Poly Studio Mobile app device list.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate to the main Device List screen.
Verify the headset shows status 'Connected'.
Power OFF the headset.
Wait 10 seconds. Verify the app shows 'Disconnected' or similar.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recent apps).
Power ON the headset.
Wait 15 seconds for the headset to connect to the phone via Bluetooth.
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to the main Device List screen.
Observe the device status.
generated_testcases.expected: The app should detect the headset as 'Connected' upon launch, since the headset was powered on and connected to the phone via Bluetooth before the app was reopened. The app should read the current Bluetooth state from the system on startup.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BT-DISCOVERY-008: Newly Paired Headset via Phone Bluetooth - App Device List Update After Bluetooth Toggle
steps: Precondition: Headset is fully charged and in pairing mode. Headset is NOT yet paired to the phone.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Navigate to the main Device List screen. Verify no headset is listed.
Minimize the app (Home button).
Open phone Settings → Bluetooth.
Pair the headset via phone Bluetooth settings. Confirm pairing is successful.
Return to Poly Studio Mobile app (bring to foreground).
Verify the headset is NOT yet visible in the device list (baseline).
Do NOT restart the app. Instead, toggle Bluetooth OFF and then ON from the phone's quick settings panel.
Wait 10 seconds.
Observe the Device List screen in the app.
generated_testcases.expected: After toggling Bluetooth off and on, the app should detect the newly paired headset and display it in the device list. If the headset still does not appear, this indicates the app only discovers devices on initial startup and does not respond to Bluetooth adapter state changes.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BT-DISCOVERY-009: Device Status Stays 'Connected' After Headset Powers Off While App is Backgrounded
steps: Precondition: Headset is paired, powered ON, and connected to the phone.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate to the main Device List screen.
Verify the headset shows status 'Connected'.
Press Home button to background the app.
Power OFF the headset.
Wait 20 seconds.
Bring Poly Studio Mobile app back to foreground.
Observe the device status on the Device List screen.
Do NOT tap any refresh or navigate away.
generated_testcases.expected: The device status should update to 'Disconnected' after the headset is powered off, even when the app was backgrounded during the power-off event. The app should detect the disconnection upon returning to the foreground.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BT-DISCOVERY-010: Device Status Update After Power On - Verify Tappable/Selectable State
steps: Precondition: Headset is paired and visible in the Poly Studio Mobile app device list.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate to the main Device List screen.
Power OFF the headset.
Wait for the device status to show 'Disconnected' or the device card to appear grayed out.
Tap on the device card/row in the device list.
Observe the app's response (e.g., does it show 'Device not available' or navigate to a details screen?).
Power ON the headset.
Wait 20 seconds for reconnection.
Observe the device status update to 'Connected'.
Tap on the device card/row again.
Verify the app navigates to the device details/settings screen (e.g., Audio Settings, Device Info).
generated_testcases.expected: When the headset is powered off and disconnected, tapping the device card should either show a 'device not available' message or gracefully handle the unavailable state. After the headset is powered on and status updates to 'Connected', tapping the device card should successfully navigate to the device settings screen without requiring an app restart.
